title: Emit Token Expiry Warning via Webhook and Response Header
description:
AI agents currently have no push signal when a token is approaching expiry. The `refresh_before` timestamp in `TokenResponse` requires the agent to implement its own timer or polling loop — a non-trivial orchestration burden that is error-prone and unnecessary if the service can proactively signal the agent.

This task adds two complementary expiry warning mechanisms:

1. **`X-Token-Expires-In` response header**: On every authenticated API response, include a header with the remaining lifetime of the access token in seconds (e.g. `X-Token-Expires-In: 120`). When this value drops below 120 seconds, additionally include `X-Token-Refresh-Advised: true`. This allows an agent that processes response headers to detect approaching expiry without any clock management.

2. **`token.expiring_soon` webhook event** (requires task 4.5): Emit a webhook event to the agent's registered webhook URL when its active token family has less than 120 seconds remaining. The event payload must include:
   - `event: "token.expiring_soon"`
   - `agent_id`
   - `family_id` (so the agent can correlate to its current token)
   - `expires_at` (ISO-8601)
   - `refresh_before` (ISO-8601)
   - `seconds_remaining` (integer)

   The threshold (120 seconds) must be configurable via server environment variable `TOKEN_EXPIRY_WARNING_SECONDS` (default: 120).

3. **`token.refreshed` webhook event**: Emit when a token family is successfully refreshed, so downstream agents that received the `expiring_soon` event know the situation has been resolved.

Note: The webhook events require that the agent has a registered `homepage_url` or a dedicated webhook endpoint field. If no webhook URL is registered, only the response header mechanism applies.
validation: Every authenticated API response includes X-Token-Expires-In header with remaining seconds
X-Token-Refresh-Advised: true header is present when remaining lifetime < TOKEN_EXPIRY_WARNING_SECONDS
X-Token-Expires-In is not present on unauthenticated responses (401, discovery, JWKS)
token.expiring_soon webhook event is emitted when token family drops below threshold
Webhook payload includes event, agent_id, family_id, expires_at, refresh_before, seconds_remaining
token.refreshed webhook event is emitted after a successful refresh token grant
TOKEN_EXPIRY_WARNING_SECONDS env var controls the threshold (default 120)
No webhook is emitted if agent has no registered webhook URL
Integration test: authenticated request with token expiring in 60s returns X-Token-Refresh-Advised: true
Integration test: authenticated request with token expiring in 800s does NOT return X-Token-Refresh-Advised
Integration test: token.expiring_soon webhook fires within 5s of crossing the threshold
